factors.summary: Overall demand is stable with favorable economic indicators.

evidence.text: The Purchasing Managers Index, published by the Institute for Supply Chain Management, averaged 55.4 in the first quarter of 2019 and 57.0 in the fourth quarter of 2018. Readings above 50 are indicative of growing demand.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, though with mixed customer responses.

evidence.text: We have implemented a number of pricing actions that are influencing the current period. [...] Price increases were aimed at mitigating the impact of product inflation in the marketplace.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Fasteners are our most cyclical product line, and daily sales grew 11.8% in the first quarter of 2019 over the first quarter of 2018. [...] We also experienced growth in sales to 81 of our top 100 customers in the first quarter of 2019.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales noted above for both 2019 and 2018 was driven primarily by higher unit sales [...] These positive factors were slightly offset by adverse weather [...] that created temporary disruptions in activity.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by both market demand and growth initiatives.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales [...] was driven primarily by higher unit sales [...] These positive factors were slightly offset by adverse weather [...] that created temporary disruptions in activity.

factors.summary: Construction end markets are growing fastest, while oil and gas markets are softening.

evidence.text: We experienced daily sales growth in our construction end market of 13.1% during the first quarter of 2019 over the first quarter of 2018. [...] The exception to this otherwise favorable backdrop is oil and gas. We do not view the softness that has emerged in oil and gas as deep by the standards of this market, but it has broadened across our regions in recent months.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly due to tariffs and inflation, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: We believe the decline in gross profit during this period is primarily due to [...] a price/cost deficit that pushes our gross profit margin lower. [...] Fourth, net rebates have not risen at the same rate as sales as we implement inventory control programs and sell through product that we accelerated into the United States from China ahead of a potential tariff increases in the fourth quarter of 2018.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher headcount and wage increases.

evidence.text: Employee-related expenses include: [...] (2) moderate increases in hourly base wages, (3) higher bonuses and commissions due to growth in net sales and net earnings, and (4) higher profit sharing expense.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are increasing to support sales growth and inflation.

evidence.text: The increase in inventory from March 31, 2018 to March 31, 2019 was primarily to support healthy business activity and sales growth, including a large increase in the number of installed vending devices and active Onsite locations, and inflation and tariffs.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

factors.summary: Management is confident with growth initiatives and market position.

evidence.text: We are a growth-centric organization focused on identifying 'drivers' that allow us to get closer to our customers and gain market share in what we believe remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Adverse weather and oil and gas market softness are near-term risks.

evidence.text: These positive factors were slightly offset by adverse weather across many of our northern regions in both periods that created temporary disruptions in activity and reduced sales by an estimated 65 to 85 basis points in the first quarter of 2019 and 50 to 70 basis points in the first quarter of 2018. [...] The exception to this otherwise favorable backdrop is oil and gas. [...] but it has broadened across our regions in recent months.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on growth through customer service and market expansion.

evidence.text: Our motto is Growth through Customer Service ® . We are a growth-centric organization focused on identifying 'drivers' that allow us to get closer to our customers and gain market share in what we believe remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Sensitivities include oil and gas market conditions and geopolitical risks.

evidence.text: The exception to this otherwise favorable backdrop is oil and gas. [...] but it has broadened across our regions in recent months. [...] Are geopolitical factors (conflicts, sanctions, trade tensions, political instability) creating material risks, disruptions, or opportunities?

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share through growth initiatives.

evidence.text: Our growth drivers have evolved and changed, and can be expected to continue to evolve and change, over time. [...] We are a growth-centric organization focused on identifying 'drivers' that allow us to get closer to our customers and gain market share in what we believe remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are manageable with a fragmented market.

evidence.text: We believe our ongoing tax rate, absent any discrete tax items, will be in the 24.5% to 25.0% range.

factors.summary: Differentiation is through customer service and market presence.

evidence.text: Our motto is Growth through Customer Service ® . [...] We are a growth-centric organization focused on identifying 'drivers' that allow us to get closer to our customers and gain market share in what we believe remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Headcount is increasing to support customer growth.

evidence.text: During the last twelve months, we increased our absolute employee headcount by 591 people in our in-market locations and 1,203 people in total. The increase is mostly a function of additions we have made to support customer growth in the field as well as investments in our growth drivers.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing to support growth initiatives.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities increased from the first quarter of 2018 to the first quarter of 2019. This was due to higher spending on property, plant and equipment. [...] We continue to anticipate net capital expenditures in 2019 to be within a range of $195.0 to $225.0, growth from 2018 of between $28.2 and $58.2, and 16.9% and 34.9%.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Investing Activities

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving due to higher net earnings and better working capital management.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased in the first quarter of 2019 relative to the first quarter of 2018, primarily due to growth in net earnings and a reduced drag from working capital investment relative to what was experienced in the first quarter of 2018.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

factors.summary: Higher interest rates are increasing net interest expense.

evidence.text: Our net interest expense was $3.9 in the first quarter of 2019 and $2.7 in the first quarter of 2018. This increase was mainly caused by higher average interest rates and a higher average debt balance during the period.

factors.summary: Currency fluctuations have a minor impact on daily sales.

evidence.text: Daily sales impact of currency fluctuations -0.5 % in 2019 and 0.5 % in 2018.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks such as trade tensions with China are impacting the business.

evidence.text: These tariffs affect a meaningful number of our products both directly as a result of the tariffs, and indirectly as a result of this action's effect on generalized inflation.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is increasing, which is leading to higher net interest expenses. Additionally, the company has authority to repurchase up to 2,400,000 shares, indicating potential capital management activity.

evidence.text: The company's net interest expense increased from $2.7 in 2018 to $3.9 in 2019, driven by higher average interest rates and a larger average debt balance.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: The company currently has authority to purchase up to 2,400,000 additional shares of its common stock.

factors.summary: The economic conditions present mixed signals with potential headwinds such as economic downturns and industry weaknesses, yet also show favorable indicators like strong GDP growth and stable demand.

evidence.text: The overall economic environment is a potential headwind due to risks like economic downturns and weakness in manufacturing and commercial construction industries.
Economic conditions are favorable with strong GDP growth and stable demand.

The Purchasing Managers Index, published by the Institute for Supply Chain Management, averaged 55.4 in the first quarter of 2019 and 57.0 in the fourth quarter of 2018. Readings above 50 are indicative of growing demand.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing margin compression, particularly in gross and operating margins, due to cost pressures and other risks such as economic downturns, competitive pressure, and changes in customer needs.

evidence.text: Our gross profit, as a percentage of net sales, was 47.7% in the first quarter of 2019 and 48.7% in the first quarter of 2018.

Our operating and administrative expenses (including the (gain) loss on sales of property and equipment), as a percentage of net sales, improved to 27.8% in the first quarter of 2019 compared to 28.9% in the first quarter of 2018.

Our forward-looking statements involve a variety of risks and uncertainties, known and unknown, and may be affected by inaccurate assumptions. Consequently, no forward-looking statement can be guaranteed and actual results may vary materially.
